title: Result - Slovenia
content:
Welcome to Slovenia, a land of exceptional natural beauty and diverse landscapes, from Alpine peaks to coastal regions, karstic caves and lush lowlands.
Climb mountains, bike through stunning terrain, or enjoy thrilling ski slopes. Explore vibrant cities, picturesque countryside and relaxing thermal spas. Dive into culture with festivals, museums and galleries, or step back in time by visiting Slovenia’s many castles and manors. Indulge in the flavours of 24 culinary regions and 3 wine-growing areas, savoring local cheeses, sausages, oils and honey. With many family-run businesses, Slovenia embodies the charm and innovation of Europe’s small enterprises.

## Unique food tradition
Idrija žlikrofi (potato dumpling): a type of pasta filled with spiced balls made of cooked potato, sautéed onions and lard.
Slovenia’s job market offers plenty of opportunities, especially in construction, hospitality, transport, manufacturing, healthcare and technology. With fewer young workers available, employers are increasingly hiring foreign talent to fill the gaps.

### Local legend or myth
The Goldenhorn: a mythical white steinbock with golden horns, said to guard great treasures atop Slovenia's Triglav Mountain. His realm is protected by White women or Vile, fairy-like beings.

### Language of the Land
Slovenian has many dialects, which are so distinct that speakers from different regions may have trouble understanding each other.
years old, the vine in Maribor is the world’s oldest grapevine, located in Slovenia.
